name: Speech Writer
description: Write keynotes, executive remarks, ceremonial speeches, conference openers, talking points, crisis or change-management addresses, and spoken rewrites that sound credible aloud, fit the speaker, and move the room under live-delivery constraints.
not_for: debate-prep war gaming or opposition research
broad communications strategy beyond the speech itself
typical_inputs: speaker identity, role, authority, and known voice traits
audience composition, mood, and what they most care about
occasion, objective, and desired takeaway or action
timing, venue, format, and whether Q&A follows
mandatory points, sensitivities, and taboo areas
source material, anecdotes, proof points, and facts that must be preserved
typical_outputs: rewritten live-language version of an existing draft
